items.title: Police tracking man linked to viral travel scam case
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police are tracking down an individual known as "Imran" to assist in investigations into an alleged travel service scam that has gone viral on social media. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said a report had been lodged in the state, prompting investigations under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating. He added that police had also received information suggesting the case might involve several states, requiring a wider probe.

items.title: Escaped Pakistani detainee case: Police probe SOP lapses, weaknesses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Police have opened an investigation paper to identify any weaknesses or non-compliance with standard operating procedures (SOP) following the escape of a Pakistani man from Sungai Buloh Prison in an incident last Thursday. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the investigation is being conducted under Sections 223 and 224 of the Penal Code, Berita Harian reported. Previously, Sungai Buloh district police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said the incident occurred at about 6pm last Thursday during the transfer of remand detainees to Sungai Buloh Prison by the Shah Alam Escort Unit.

items.title: Platform barriers, warning systems under consideration for LRT stations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is considering upgrading safety features at Light Rail Transit (LRT) stations, including platform barriers and other warning systems, following recent incidents involving passengers falling onto the tracks. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said discussions had been held with LRT operator Prasarana Malaysia Bhd, which has proposed installing platform screen doors at stations to enhance passenger safety. "So what we have done is hold discussions with Prasarana, and they have already put forward proposals to install platform screen doors at our stations.

items.title: 'We're not being wasteful': PN defends motorcycle convoy as fundraiser, road safety drive
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has defended the motorcycle convoy it recently organised, which saw four menteris besar from Pas-led states participate, saying it was not wasteful amid the ongoing energy crisis. PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the convoy was an initiative to promote responsible and safe motorcycle riding. PKR deputy president Nurul Izzah Anwar recently criticised PN's motorcycle convoy as tone-deaf and wasteful amid the energy crisis.

items.title: Court grants FashionValet founder Vivy Yusof, husband temporary release of passport to perform Haj, trial postponed to July
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — The trial involving FashionValet Sdn Bhd (FashionValet) founder Datin Vivy Yusof and her husband, Datuk Fadzarudin Shah Anuar, for criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving RM8 million in investment funds, which was supposed to begin today, has been postponed to July. Sessions Court Judge Rosli Ahmad postponed the trial following an application by the couple’s counsel, Shaarvin Raaj, to vacate the earlier trial dates scheduled today, April 14 to 17, May 18 to 22 and June 8 to 12, 2026, as they need to prepare their defence statements. On December 5, 2024, Vivy, whose full name is Vivy Sofinas Yusof, 38, and Fadzarudin, 37, pleaded not guilty to charges of CBT involving funds from Khazanah Nasional Berhad and Permodalan Nasional Berhad amounting to RM8 million.

items.title: Tighter exports, biodiesel demand to support CPO prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Crude palm oil (CPO) prices are expected to remain well supported amid tightening regional export supply, as Thailand and Indonesia prioritise domestic biodiesel programmes, alongside weather risks linked to a developing El Niño. Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) said Malaysia's palm oil inventories are likely to edge towards the psychological two million tonne level over the next two months. On regional developments, Thailand is tightening palm oil exports to safeguard domestic supply.

items.title: Three Myanmar nationals remanded further 7 days in countryman's murder case
items.summary: Magistrate Savinder Singh Jugindar Singh issued the extension order at the magistrate's court here today to facilitate further probes under Section 302 of the Penal Code. The suspects — two men aged 37 and 38, and a 37-year-old woman — arrived at the court at 8.30am under police escort. Their previous remand period ended today.

items.title: E-hailing ride turns drug run: Johor cops arrest mule in Pontian bust
items.summary: PONTIAN, April 13 — Police have uncovered a drug distribution tactic involving the use of e-hailing services following the arrest of a drug mule during an operation last Friday. Pontian police chief Supt Hadzrat Hussien Mion Hussain said a team from the District Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division (BSJND), conducting an Ops Tapis patrol, saw a suspicious e-hailing car by the roadside along Jalan Kukup, Kampung Penerok, at about 9.15pm and approached it. “A 36-year-old unemployed local man, who is a passenger in the car, was arrested to facilitate the investigation,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Two feared trapped as fire guts George Town shop house
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Two people are believed to be trapped in a fire that engulfed a double-storey shophouse along King Street here today. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said they received a distress call at 12.14pm. Firefighters from the Lebuh Pantai Fire and Rescue Station, with assistance from the Jalan Perak station, arrived at the scene at 12.25pm.

items.title: Sime Darby Property sees strong take-up at Elmina park
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd said its latest industrial development, Elmina Business Park 2, has seen encouraging demand since its launch in January, marking its expansion into Rawang in the northern Klang Valley. Located about two kilometres from the existing Elmina Business Park, the 242.81-hactare (ha) development has a gross development value of RM1.72 billion. The broader Elmina Business Park, spanning 607.03ha, is arguably the Klang Valley's largest freehold industrial business park.

items.title: Malaysia leads Asia in legacy planning among wealthy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is emerging as one of the more proactive markets in Asia for legacy planning among high net worth individuals, according to HSBC Life's inaugural survey covering Asia and the Middle East. The report, titled "Bridging the intentions-action gap", found that 52 per cent of high net worth individuals in Malaysia have a formal legacy plan, above the survey average of 41 per cent. This places Malaysia ahead of several more developed wealth hubs, including Singapore (45 per cent), Hong Kong (26 per cent) and Taiwan (24 per cent).

items.title: [UPDATED] Job scam call centre targeting Japan busted in Seri Kembangan, nine held
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have crippled a job scam syndicate operating a call centre from a residence Seri Kembangan, Selangor, following a raid on April 8. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the operation was carried out by the Serdang Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) with assistance from social media analysts from the Petaling Jaya police. He said investigations revealed the syndicate was involved in online job scams targeting victims in Japan.

items.title: RM4.2mil in drugs seized at KLIA, 4 arrested
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have dismantled two drug trafficking syndicates at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA), seizing narcotics worth more than RM4.2 million and arresting four suspects in a series of operations earlier this month. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the largest seizure involved 45kg of cannabis valued at RM3.6 million, believed to have been destined for the international market, particularly Europe. "The drugs were detected on April 6 when Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad auxiliary police intercepted three suspicious pieces of luggage.

items.title: Lorry driver charged with parang attack in Bera
items.summary: TEMERLOH: A lorry driver was charged at the Sessions Court here today with slashing a man with a parang in Bera last month. The accused is alleged to have intentionally caused grievous hurt to the 35-year-old victim using a weapon capable of causing death. Deputy public prosecutor Khairunnisa Zaffar proposed bail of RM8,000 with one surety, informing the court that the accused has a prior record related to drug offences.

items.title: ‘Even burger stalls look better’: Home minister orders upgrades for Malaysia’s border posts
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 13 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail today instructed several Malaysia’s border posts to be upgraded as some are currently in a state that is less presentable than even a burger stall. He said he personally inspected several border checkpoints yesterday in Kedah and Perak, including Padang Besar, Bukit Kayu Hitam, Durian Burung and Pengkalan Hulu, and found that some duty posts used by security forces were in poor condition. Even a burger stall looks nicer,” he said in his speech during his ministry’s monthly assembly.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police hunt woman who abandoned RM1.2mil drugs at KLIA
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police are hunting a woman who fled the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) after abandoning a bag containing more than RM1.2 million worth of methamphetamine. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the drugs were detected on March 30 during routine baggage scanning by Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd auxiliary police. Those with information can contact Inspector Rohaya Razali at 019-768 6165, the Selangor NCID hotline at 03-5514 5308 or the Selangor police operations room at 03-5520 2222.

items.title: Don’t panic, don’t relax: Home minister urges Malaysians to brace for possible energy-driven shocks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 13 — Malaysians should prepare for possible economic and supply disruptions if the ongoing geopolitical conflict in West Asia continues to affect global energy routes, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. “But we do not want the public to panic. Saifuddin Nasution said the government’s priority remains protecting public welfare and ensuring Malaysians do not immediately feel the full impact of external shocks.

items.title: Syed Hamid Albar mulls political comeback to Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno Supreme Council member Tan Sri Syed Hamid Albar says he is "seeking inspiration" on whether to return to Umno after leaving the party in 2018. Syed Hamid, who later joined Bersatu before announcing his retirement from politics in 2022, described Umno as the best party for Malays compared to others. Among those reported to be considering a return to Umno are former vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein, former Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin, and former Jempol Umno division chief Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Mohd Sharif.

items.title: [UPDATED] Cops: Possible SOP breach in Sungai Buloh Prison detainee escape
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have opened an internal investigation into possible breaches of standard operating procedures (SOP) following the escape of a remand detainee from the Sungai Buloh Prison. Last week, a Pakistani detainee escaped during a transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison after attending court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court on April 9. He confirmed that statements have been recorded from multiple parties, including police personnel, prison officers and detainees who witnessed the incident.

items.title: Trump rejects Pope Leo's appeal for peace, calls him 'liberal'
items.summary: JOINT BASE ANDREWS, Maryland: US President Donald Trump told reporters Sunday that he is "not a big fan" of Pope Leo XIV, after the global leader of Catholics made a plea for peace. "I'm not a big fan of Pope Leo. On Friday, a Vatican official denied reports that a top Pentagon official gave the church's envoy to the United States a "bitter lecture" over Pope Leo's criticisms of the Trump administration.

items.title: [UPDATED] Probe into 12 Kajang police personnel ongoing, awaiting technical reports
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Investigations into 12 police personnel arrested in Kajang over a cryptocurrency robbery are nearing completion, but remain pending due to outstanding technical reports. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the case has yet to be referred to the deputy public prosecutor as authorities are awaiting input from relevant technical agencies, particularly those involving cryptocurrency transactions. "The investigation is almost complete, but we are still waiting for several technical reports, especially those related to cryptocurrency transactions," he told reporters at the Selangor police headquarters today.

items.title: Unpaid sales tax lands two in court: Penang directors face charges over RM16,000 in arrears
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 13 — Two directors of a company involved in the manufacturing and marketing of prayer items were charged at the Sessions Court today with 18 counts of failing to pay sales tax amounting to more than RM16,000. According to the charges, both individuals, as directors of KVS Industries Sdn Bhd, are accused of failing to pay sales tax for 18 taxable periods between March 1, 2021 and December 31, 2023, involving total arrears and penalties of RM16,145.44. According to the charges, Moganadass and Saroja Devi, who are directors of KVS Industries Sdn Bhd, are accused of failing to pay sales tax for 18 taxable periods between March 1, 2021 and December 31, 2023, involving a total arrears of sales tax and penalties of RM16,145.44.

items.title: Fewer people buying new cars after Aidilfitri as global uncertainty dents confidence, says industry group
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 13 — The ongoing conflict in West Asia is expected to weigh on Malaysia’s automotive industry as demand for new vehicles shows a slight dip, according to industry players. Malaysian Malay Vehicle Importers and Traders Association (Pekema) president Datuk Mohamed Nazari Noordin said the softer demand trend was first observed after the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period and has been influenced by global geopolitical uncertainty, Harian Metro reported today. “There is a slight drop in demand for new cars, especially after Hari Raya, and the West Asia conflict has also affected buyer confidence,” he said.

items.title: MMEA foils RM1 million syabu smuggling attempt off Kota Tinggi
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) foiled an attempt to smuggle 20kg of syabu worth RM1 million near Batu Layar on April 8. Tanjung Sedili Maritime Zone acting director Maritime Commander Mohd Najib Sam said a patrol boat intercepted the shipment at 9.40pm about 0.4 nautical miles northwest of Batu Layar. "Our enforcement officers were on routine duty when they detected a suspicious, unregistered fibreglass boat heading towards a neighbouring country.

items.title: Supporting farmers an investment in national stability
items.summary: LETTERS: Recent warnings that farmers may stop planting due to rising diesel costs are symptoms of a deeper, longstanding failure in how the country's food system is structured. The real issue lies in a system where those who produce food are the least rewarded. Subsidies must prioritise food production that benefits the many.

items.title: NexG clarifies losses, says concerns misplaced
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: NexG Bhd said concerns over its financial position, investment decisions and governance practices stem from misinterpretation of information that had already been disclosed to the market. The company said losses linked to its investment were due to fair value adjustments, which are non-cash accounting entries required under Malaysian Financial Reporting Standards. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Grieving aunt willing to forgive teacher in fatal school car accident
items.summary: Zuraifah Noor, 43, who had cared for Nur Fatimatul Hawa Mohd Azaudin for the past five years, said the family remains in shock and is not yet ready to consider legal action. However, I leave it entirely to the victim's father and other family members. Zuraifah added that she briefly met the teacher's family at the district police station yesterday while lodging a report.

items.title: Inspace Creation designs its way to ACE Market, to debut on May 8
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Interior fitting-out services provider Inspace Creation Bhd has launched its prospectus for an initial public offering (IPO) ahead of its ACE Market debut on Bursa Malaysia on May 8. The IPO will raise RM17.13 million through the issuance of 68.50 million new ordinary shares at 25 sen each, alongside an offer for sale of 29.30 million existing shares, the company said at the launch today. Of the proceeds, RM6 million is allocated for capital expenditure, RM4.39 million for working capital, RM2.74 million for bank borrowings repayment, and RM4 million for listing expenses.

items.title: Kelana Jaya LRT line fully operational after early morning track switch disruption at Universiti station
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Train services on the Kelana Jaya LRT line have resumed normal operations following an early morning disruption caused by a track switch fault at Universiti station today. Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd said full services were restored at 7.21am after initial repair works were completed by its engineering team. “However, engineering and operations teams will continue to monitor train movements throughout operating hours to ensure smooth service on the Kelana Jaya line,” Rapid Rail said in a statement.

items.title: TNB pushes towards 70pct renewable energy capacity by 2050
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) is actively operationalising its roadmap to achieve 70 per cent renewable energy (RE) capacity by 2050, chief sustainability officer Leo Pui Yong said. TNB's energy evolution also serves as a regional anchor, Leo said. In a major step toward the ASEAN Power Grid (APG), TNB has operationalised ENEGEM (Energy Exchange Malaysia).

items.title: 'Work smart,' Saifuddin tells police on fuel smuggling enforcement
items.summary: He said although it was previously announced that 80 petrol stations would be monitored, the ministry has since doubled that figure to 170 stations located near Malaysian borders . Saifuddin said police must work with the Finance Ministry and Domestic Trade Ministry to check sales data for suspicious volumes. We must work smart.

items.title: Malaysian's second shot at wedded bliss ends in immigration, bigamy bust
items.summary: He is also accused of attempting to marry another Indonesian woman despite still being legally married. Authorities said Zukri first entered Indonesia in 2014 and married an Indonesian woman in Salatiga, Central Java, but never formally dissolved the marriage. Ponorogo Immigration chief Anggoro Widy Utomo said investigations found that Zukri's passport had expired, constituting a violation of immigration laws.

items.title: Busted at sea: Two tankers held over illicit diesel transfer off Penang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Malaysia’s Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said it has detained two tankers for allegedly conducting an illegal ship-to-ship transfer of about 700,000 litres of diesel off the island of Penang over the weekend. Penang MMEA director Muhammad Suffi Mohd Ramli said authorities carried out checks following an intelligence tip about the ships, which were anchored in the waters off Bagan Ajam on Saturday. The alleged transfer involved about 700,000 litres of Euro 5 diesel, while the total amount seized was estimated at around 800,000 litres, with a value of about RM5.43 million (US$1.37 million), he said.

items.title: Family of schoolgirl killed in Kelantan crash still in shock, leave legal action to authorities
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 13 — The family of the 13-year-old girl killed in a school crash in Pengkalan Chepa yesterday says they are still in shock and are not ready to decide on any legal action. Zuraifah Noor, the victim’s aunt and main guardian for the past five years, said the family is leaving the matter entirely to the authorities, Malay daily BH reported today. The 43-year-old woman said the family is still struggling to process what happened and does not want to interfere with ongoing investigations.

items.title: Illegal route, valid IDs: Kelantan police confirm status of 34 Sungai Golok-crossing students
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 13 — Police have confirmed that all 34 primary and secondary school students who entered Malaysia from Thailand yesterday possess valid Malaysian identity cards. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said all of them have been released on police bail with two Malaysian sureties. Mohd Yusoff said investigations found that the group of students reside around Sungai Golok, Thailand, but used illegal routes along the Malaysia-Thailand border to attend schools in the Rantau Panjang and Pasir Mas areas.

items.title: Bukit Aman warns of scams targeting victims with fake money recovery offers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bukit Aman has warned of a new scam tactic targeting previous fraud victims, where syndicates pose as authorities offering to recover lost funds only to deceive them a second time. Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the modus operandi typically begins with victims being lured by Facebook advertisements before being contacted via WhatsApp by suspects impersonating National Scam Response Centre (NSRC) representatives. "The suspects would then claim they could help recover money lost in earlier scams, but would require victims to make upfront payments as part of the so-called service.

items.title: Pay more, lose more: Police warn of ‘double scam’ trapping victims with fake recovery offers
items.summary: Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (JSJK) director Rusdi Mohd Isa said the syndicate’s tactic involves impersonating representatives of the National Scam Response Centre (NSRC) to deceive victims. Rusdi said the syndicate promotes its so-called recovery services through advertisements on Facebook before approaching victims via WhatsApp. Rusdi said victims, often desperate to recover their losses, are then instructed to make multiple payments to different bank accounts.

items.title: Senior pathologist tells inquest Zara's injuries consistent with fall from height
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A senior forensic pathologist has concurred with findings that 13-year-old Zara Qairina Mahathir's fatal injuries were consistent with a fall from height. Datuk Seri Dr Bhupinder Singh Jeswant Singh, 74, testified before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan today in the inquest into the teenager's death. Bhupinder, an associate professor in forensic and legal medicine at the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland & University College Dublin Malaysia Campus (RUMC) in Penang, was the 68th witness called by the legal team led by Datuk Ram Singh.

items.title: Kota Bharu teacher, 57, remanded two days over crash that killed Form One girl at school surau
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 13 — A 57-year-old female teacher has been remanded for two days, from today, to assist in investigations into an accident that claimed the life of a female student at the surau of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Tanjong Mas here yesterday. The remand order was issued by Magistrate Wan Mohd I’zzat Wan Abdullah at the Magistrate’s Court here to facilitate investigations under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987. Kota Bharu police chief ACP Mohd Azmir Damiri said initial investigations found that the driver is believed to have mistakenly pressed the accelerator pedal before losing control and hitting the victim.

items.title: Home Ministry staff warned over social media posts during WFH periods
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Home Ministry staff have been reminded against posting social media content that suggests they are "slacking off" during work-from-home arrangements. Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Awang Alik Jeman said the ministry would not hesitate to take disciplinary action against those found portraying a lifestyle of truancy while on duty. Awang Alik said the reminder was intended to safeguard staff careers and uphold discipline within the ministry.

items.title: Stronger scrap metal oversight needed to curb MRT cable theft [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Stronger oversight of scrap metal transactions is crucial to curb Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) cable theft and other infrastructure-related crimes, says Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye. His remarks come amid continued cable theft incidents disrupting MRT services in the Klang Valley, despite heightened security measures. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Japan's Nikkei falls as US-Iran peace talks falter
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's Nikkei share average slipped one per cent on Monday as the collapse of US-Iran peace talks and reports of a US Navy plan to block the Strait of Hormuz cast fresh doubts on the durability of an ongoing ceasefire. The broader Topix slid 0.5 per cent in choppy trading to 3,721.78. Across the Tokyo Stock Exchange's 33 industry sub-indexes, energy exploration stocks stood out with a 3.2 per cent gain.

items.title: Oil leaps, dollar firm and stocks wobble as US-Iran peace talks collapse
items.summary: SINGAPORE/LONDON: Oil prices surged on Monday as the US moved to impose a blockade on Iranian shipping after the collapse of weekend peace talks, while the dollar rose and stocks and bonds fell in Asia ahead of the start of US earnings season later in the day. Brent crude futures were up 7.3 per cent at US$102 a barrel. S&P 500 futures were down 0.7 per cent through the Asia morning and European futures fell 1.3 per cent.

items.title: Roadworks nationwide hit by rising costs, with hundreds of projects already delayed, says Ahmad Maslan
items.summary: KULAI, April 13 — Road construction and maintenance projects nationwide are expected to face further delays due to rising construction material prices and diesel costs linked to tensions in West Asia, Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said today. He said the government expects the number of infrastructure projects already behind schedule to increase if current cost pressures continue. He named the Malaysian Bumiputera Contractors Association, the Malaysian Malay Contractors Association, Class F Bumiputera Contractors Association and the Mechanical Contractors Association.

items.title: Joint Malaysia–Indonesia team rescues two fishermen drifting into Malaysian waters off Kukup
items.summary: JAKARTA, April 13 — Two Indonesian fishermen who drifted into Malaysian waters after their boat suffered engine failure have been safely rescued by a joint search and rescue (SAR) operation with assistance from the Malaysian authorities. He said the rescue involved coordination between Indonesian authorities, including the Karimun Police Water Unit, and Malaysia’s Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre (MRCC) Johor to facilitate evacuation efforts in Malaysian waters. Following approval from Malaysian authorities, a rescue team from the Tanjung Balai Karimun SAR Post departed at 5pm local time on Sunday aboard a Polair speedboat and reached the victims at 6.53pm in Malaysian waters.

items.title: Posting about badminton or shopping during WFH? Our eyes are on you, Home Ministry sec-gen warns staff
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 13 — Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman today warned civil servants against posting photos or updates on social media that may suggest they are neglecting their duties while working from home (WFH), saying disciplinary action will be taken against offenders. He said officers must not upload content such as leisure activities, including sports or outings, that could be perceived as being absent from work during the WFH period. Awang said department heads must ensure essential services such as identity card counters remain operational and are not disrupted by WFH arrangements.

items.title: Look out for unusual fuel sales to curb smuggling, home ministry tells cops on border petrol station duty
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 13 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail today told the police to use data to spot suspicious activity at petrol stations near Malaysia’s borders, focusing on outlets where fuel sales spike unusually or appear unreasonable. He said such anomalies would allow enforcement agencies to zoom in on specific locations and increase monitoring, as part of a targeted approach to curb smuggling. “I suggest obtaining accurate data from the Ministry of Finance and KPDN on the sales volume of each petrol station near the border.

items.title: Festive week ahead for Malaysia with Tamil New Year, Vaisakhi and Vishu celebrations from tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Tamil and Sikh communities will celebrate Tamil New Year and Vaisakhi, tomorrow (April 14), while the Malayalee community will observe Vishu on April 15, reflecting the spirit of unity and shared values among Malaysia’s diverse communities. The Telugu community celebrated their new year, Ugadi, on March 19, further reflecting the diversity of cultural celebrations in Malaysia. Its president, T. Ganesan, said the new year should inspire the community to reflect on traditions, safeguard values, uphold rights, and strengthen unity.

items.title: LRT disruption: Kelana Jaya line hit by delays after Universiti station fault, Rapid Rail activates shuttle service
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Train services on the Kelana Jaya LRT line are experiencing delays following a track switch disruption at Universiti station, operator Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd said today. In a statement, Rapid Rail said the disruption has affected the train schedule along the line, leading to longer waiting times for passengers at platforms. “Due to this operational disruption, passengers are expected to experience longer waiting times on platforms,” Rapid Rail said in a statement.

items.title: Trip to Songkran festival ends with drug test trouble for two SPM candidates at Bukit Kayu Hitam
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM, April 13 — Two 17-year-old Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) candidates were among 37 individuals detained after testing positive for drugs in a special operation by the National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK) Kedah branch at the Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex here yesterday. In the first case, a male SPM candidate and his 21-year-old sister were detained upon returning from Danok, Thailand, after attending the Songkran festival there. “Test results found that the sister was positive for three types of drugs, namely methamphetamine, MDMA and ketamine, while her younger brother, who is sitting for the SPM this year, tested positive for ketamine,” he said.

items.title: Part-time job ad on Facebook leads Sarawakian in Kuching to lose close to RM39,000
items.summary: Sarawak United People’s Party (SUPP) Public Complaints Bureau chief Milton Foo said after the victim clicked on the advertisement on April 1, she was directed to a customer service representative via WhatsApp and instructed to log into a supposed work account to carry out tasks, which involved booking hotel orders. “After the platform’s so-called financial department confirmed the tasks, the work remuneration would supposedly be remitted into her bank account,” he said in a statement Saturday. Foo said the victim made 13 online transfers within just a few days, amounting to nearly RM39,000.

items.title: Not just Bangkok: Tourism minister wants more direct flights to Thailand’s cities and tourist spots
items.summary: BANGKOK, April 13 — The Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC) urged the development of flight routes between Malaysia and Thailand to be expanded more strategically, rather than focusing solely on the main Bangkok-Kuala Lumpur route. Its minister, Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing, said the proposal-which also includes introducing more new destinations to stimulate bilateral tourism growth-was raised during his meeting with Thai Airways and Thai Vietjet Air during his recent working visit to Bangkok. In addition, Tiong also proposed that direct flight connectivity be strengthened between cities in northern Thailand, such as Chiang Mai and Chiang Rai, and Malaysia.

items.title: Man, 69, dies after encounter with wild elephant in Lahad Datu plantation
items.summary: LAHAD DATU, April 13 — A 69-year-old man died after he was believed to have been trampled by a wild elephant in an incident at Begahak 2 Plantation, Jalan Tungku, here yesterday. Lahad Datu deputy police chief Supt Jimmy Panyau said that initial investigations found that the victim and his 38-year-old son were at the oil palm plantation when a wild elephant suddenly appeared. According to him, after the incident, the victim’s son rushed his father to Lahad Datu Hospital for treatment.

items.title: Fire engulfs three blocks at Bachok religious school, no casualties; classes go online till Thursday
items.summary: BACHOK, April 13 — Three building blocks at Sekolah Menengah Ugama (A) Darul Ulum Muhammadiah, Kolam, Pak Badol here caught fire yesterday afternoon. Bachok Fire and Rescue Station senior officer Khairul Arifin Ramli said the buildings suffered 98 per cent, 80 per cent and 30 per cent damage respectively because of the fire, but no casualties were reported. “We received a distress call at 3.37 pm and the fire engine arrived at the location, about 12 kilometres away, at 3.56 pm “Firefighting teams from Ketereh, Pasir Tumboh and Pengkalan Chepa assisted the operation to put out the fire, with 25 personnel involved,” he said in a statement yesterday, adding that the fire was brought under control at 4.10 pm and that the operation was still ongoing to ensure that no fire source remained to cause a recurrence.

items.title: Kelantan school tragedy: Anwar extends condolences, says incident a lesson to be vigilant against negligence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — The tragedy that claimed the life of a Form One schoolgirl at SMK Tanjong Mas, Kota Bharu, Kelantan, yesterday, should serve as a lesson for all to be careful and avoid negligence, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a post on X yesterday, he also expressed his condolences to the family of Nur Fatimatul Hawa Mohd Azaudin and prayed for the speedy recovery of another victim who was injured. Earlier, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek was reported to have said that the Ministry of Education (MOE) had initiated an internal investigation process to review compliance with all standard operating procedures (SOPs) following the incident.

items.title: MOE probes SOP compliance after Form One student killed in SMK Tanjong Mas crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) has started an internal investigation to review compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs) following an accident that claimed the life of a Form One schoolgirl at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Tanjong Mas, Kota Bharu, Kelantan yesterday. Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the case is also being investigated by the authorities, while the State Education Department (JPN) is organising assistance for the family of the victim, Nur Fatimatul Hawa Mohd Azaudin. Earlier, Kota Bharu district police chief ACP Mohd Azmir Damiri said the student died after being hit by a car in front of the girls’ surau at SMK Tanjong Mas yesterday.

items.title: Amid strict enforcement, Malaysia detects thousands of drink‑driving offences yearly as fatal cases remain in low double digits
items.summary: While the offence remains a serious safety concern, enforcement data shows authorities maintain strong action against it and that fatal drink driving cases make up a small share of Malaysia’s overall road deaths, Alcohol-related crashes have accounted for less than 0.5 per cent of total fatalities annually in recent years, with figures consistently remaining low. In absolute terms, fatal drink-driving cases have stayed in the low double digits, with 13 cases recorded in 2023 and 12 in 2024. The government had strengthened its legal approach to drink driving in October 2020 through amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Pharmacists call for buffer stock and stronger local production to secure Malaysia’s medicine supply
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — The move to require pharmaceutical companies to report potential medicine supply shortages at least six months in advance, starting July 1, has been described as proactive and capable of strengthening the country’s supply amid global uncertainty. Malaysian Pharmacists Society (MPS) president Prof Amrahi Buang said that the measure must be supported by comprehensive policies, including buffer stock and the strengthening of local production. He said the issue of medicine supply security must be taken seriously, drawing lessons from the Covid-19 pandemic, when supplies of basic medicines such as paracetamol faced significant shortages.

items.title: Driver who fatally struck Form One girl in Kota Bharu hit accelerator by mistake, say police
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 12 — A Form One schoolgirl was killed while another was injured after they were hit by a car in their school compound when the driver unintentionally stepped on the accelerator. Kota Bharu district police chief ACP Mohd Azmir Damiri said police received a report on the fatal crash at the girls’ surau of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Tanjong Mas at about 2.15pm. He also urged eyewitnesses to come forward and assist investigations by contacting traffic investigating officer Insp Muhammad Syarafuddin Ramlee at 09-7752315 or the Kota Bharu district police headquarters Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division.

items.title: Perikatan to inform Dewan Rakyat Speaker opposition leader seat now vacant, says Samsuri
items.summary: According to Free Malaysia Today , PN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said coalition secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan has been tasked with preparing the letter, which will be submitted tomorrow as today is a non-working day. This comes following reports that Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin has stepped down from the position. Ahmad Samsuri said PN aims to name a new opposition leader before Parliament reconvenes in June.
